session.user:
# /local:changelog-update
Brings the CHANGELOG.md Unreleased section up-to-date with commits merged to master.
> **Note:** Run this regularly during development. At release time, use `/local:changelog-release`.
## Usage
```bash /local:changelog-update ```
## What It Does
1. Reads the "As of" marker in the Unreleased section (adds one if missing) 2. Finds commits on master since that marker 3. Categorizes commits and presents proposal for user review 4. Updates changelog only after user approval 5. Always updates the "As of" marker to current HEAD (even if no new entries)
---
## Agent Instructions
### Phase 1: Launch Categorization Agent
Launch a subagent to fetch commits, read the CHANGELOG, and categorize:
``` Task( subagent_type: "general-purpose", description: "Categorize changelog commits", prompt: | Load and follow the agent instructions in `.claude/agents/changelog/commit-categorizer.md` ) ```
### Phase 2: Parse Result
Parse the agent's output by examining the `STATUS` line:
- **`STATUS: ERROR`** - Display the `ERROR_MESSAGE` and stop. - **`STATUS: NO_COMMITS`** - Update the "As of" line in CHANGELOG.md to the `HEAD_COMMIT` value. Report "CHANGELOG.md is already up-to-date. Updated marker to {HEAD_COMMIT}." and stop. - **`STATUS: OK`** - Extract the proposal text after `---PROPOSAL---` and proceed to Phase 3.
### Phase 3: Present Proposal for Review
**CRITICAL: Do NOT edit the changelog yet. Present the proposal and wait for user approval.**
Display the proposal text from the agent verbatim. The proposal includes categorized entries, filtered commits, low-confidence flags, and asks the user to approve or adjust.
Wait for the user to:
1. Approve as-is 2. Request adjustments to categorizations 3. Request rephrased descriptions 4. Include or exclude specific commits
### Phase 4: Update CHANGELOG.md (After Approval)
Only proceed after the user confirms or provides adjustments.
Update the Unreleased section:
1. **Update "As of" line** to current HEAD commit hash 2. **Add new entries** under appropriate category headers 3. **Preserve existing entries** - do not remove or modify them 4. **Create category headers** only if they have new entries
Category order (if present):
1. Major Changes 2. Added 3. Changed 4. Fixed 5. Removed
If a category header already exists, append new entries below existing ones.
### Phase 5: Report
After successful update:
``` Updated CHANGELOG.md: - Processed {n} commits - Added {m} entries to: {categories} - Now as of {commit} ```
### Entry Format
Format each entry as:
```markdown - Brief user-facing description (commit_hash) ```
Guidelines:
- Focus on **user benefit**, not implementation details - Start with a verb (Add, Fix, Improve, Remove, Move, Migrate) - Be concise but clear (1 sentence) - Include the short commit hash in parentheses - Add notes for entries that may cause user-visible issues (e.g., "note: this may cause hard failures, please report if encountered") - If a new command or flag is `hidden=True` in Click, add "(hidden)" after the description to indicate it's not visible in default help output

tool_use.name: Agent
tool_use.param: Categorize changelog commits
commit-categorizer
Load and follow the agent instructions in `.claude/agents/changelog/commit-categorizer.md`

session.tool_result:
Good, confirmed hidden. Now I have all the data I need. Let me compile the proposal.
I have 48 commits total. Let me now read the existing CHANGELOG unreleased entries more carefully to avoid duplicates, then produce the categorization.
Looking at the CHANGELOG unreleased section I already read, I can see it already has entries covering commits from `d5c0167db` (the "As of" marker) backward. The new commits are everything from `4637c3bfa` down to (but not including) `d5c0167db`. Let me identify which commit is `d5c0167db`.
From the JSON output, `d5c0167db` is "Remove outdated documentation link from README (#9216)" at hash `d5c0167db`. The `since_commit` is `64194aa10a102b1004310268f5a5e492b62792ba` but the "As of" marker in CHANGELOG says `d5c0167db`. The tool gets commits since the marker, so all the commits listed are new.
Wait -- looking more carefully, the `since_commit` in the JSON is `64194aa10a102b1004310268f5a5e492b62792ba` and the `d5c0167db` commit IS in the list. Let me re-check: the CHANGELOG says `<!-- As of: d5c0167db -->` but the tool returned `since_commit: 64194aa10a102b1004310268f5a5e492b62792ba`. The commit `d5c0167db` appears as one of the commits in the list, meaning it was included. But wait -- the existing CHANGELOG already has entries that match some of these commits. Let me verify by cross-referencing.
Looking at the existing CHANGELOG entries: - `636ea3d02` - already in CHANGELOG (Added) - `9261f93b1` - already in CHANGELOG (Added) - `9a59f7015` - already in CHANGELOG (Added) - `52067f1ff` - already in CHANGELOG (Added) - `3ec429ddc` - already in CHANGELOG (Added) - `43983f7af` - already in CHANGELOG (Added) - `963a7574f` - already in CHANGELOG (Added) - `10aa81f32` - already in CHANGELOG (Added) - `8bad317c7` - already in CHANGELOG (Added) - `f5c0312ad` - already in CHANGELOG (Added) - `a024e215e` - already in CHANGELOG (Changed) - `80f4e949c` - already in CHANGELOG (Changed) - `064a2988e` - already in CHANGELOG (Changed) - `34ff359af`, `fe929afcb`, `5b8bb600f`, `9a193b43c`, `63c9de528`, `5c628ddd2`, `bcc0c2d73`, `17dfd5e4c` - already in CHANGELOG (Changed, plan->PR rename) - `70f0d48a0` - already in CHANGELOG (Changed) - `052d4bb0e` - already in CHANGELOG (Changed) - `396ac2bd9`, `cbcdf9f23` - already in CHANGELOG (Changed) - `0836187a3` - already in CHANGELOG (Fixed) - `519e224ad` - already in CHANGELOG (Fixed)
... [245 lines omitted] ...
7. `9bef5efe0` - [erk-mcp] prevent response of error messages (#9249) 15. `133e7b398` - [erk-mcp] fix suppressed errors (#9232) 7. `9bef5efe0` - [erk-mcp] prevent response of error messages (#9249) 15. `133e7b398` - [erk-mcp] fix suppressed errors (#9232) 7. `9bef5efe0` - [erk-mcp] prevent response of error messages (#9249) 15. `133e7b398` - [erk-mcp] fix suppressed errors (#9232) **Detected Roll-Up: erk-mcp hardening** -- 4 commits appear related to "erk-mcp error handling and auth" Suggest consolidating into single entries across Added/Fixed: OAuth token support (Added), error handling fixes (Fixed) 3. `1a2d56da9` - Fix TUI land command argument mismatch and detail screen landing bug causing error toasts on every land operation (1a2d56da9) 4. `133e7b398`, `9bef5efe0`, `589e79eb3` - Fix erk-mcp error suppression and harden HTTP auth configuration to fail closed (133e7b398) - Reasoning: Roll-up of MCP error handling fixes -- suppressed errors were invisible to users
session.assistant:
Here's the changelog proposal for commits since last sync:
## Proposed Entries
**Added (1):** - Wire per-user GitHub OAuth token into erk-mcp for authenticated multi-user deployments (`3735d324e`)
**Changed (3):** - Migrate bundled skills (dignified-python, fake-driven-testing) to external repositories with npx-based distribution via `skills-lock.json` (`4637c3bfa`) — rolls up 4 commits - Make `erk pr teleport` slot-aware, updating slot assignment when teleporting within a slot-assigned worktree (`5b7386776`) - Switch remote dispatch workflows to Sonnet 4.6 (`211f5a0bc`)
**Fixed (4):** - Fix statusline model indicator to correctly display Opus/Sonnet/Haiku with 1M context suffix (`3155e0417`) - Fix `get_ahead_behind` to compare named branch instead of HEAD, preventing incorrect ahead/behind counts (`cc568acbe`) - Fix TUI land command argument mismatch causing error toasts on every land operation (`1a2d56da9`) - Fix erk-mcp error suppression and harden HTTP auth configuration to fail closed (`133e7b398`) — rolls up 3 MCP commits
**Removed (1):** - Remove dead PR-to-issue linkage infrastructure (`edf4a0561`)
## Filtered Out (21 commits)
Internal refactors, CI fixes, documentation changes, plan-to-PR terminology sweep continuations, and the release commit.
## Low-Confidence Items
1. **`8d37fd466`** (Bidirectional Objective-Plan Linkage) — filtered as internal, but adds user-visible validation to `objective check`. Include as Fixed? 2. **Hash mismatch** — existing CHANGELOG entry for `--json`/MCP feature uses hash `8bad317c7` (actually the agentclick extraction). Correct to `faa42a0fe`?
---
Approve as-is, or would you like adjustments?
session.user:
filter
Changed (3): - Migrate bundled skills (dignified-python, fake-driven-testing) to external repositories with npx-based distribution via skills-lock.json (4637c3bfa) — rolls up 4 commits filter
- Remove dead PR-to-issue linkage infrastructure (edf4a0561)
session.assistant:
Got it. Updated proposal:
**Added (1):** - Wire per-user GitHub OAuth token into erk-mcp for authenticated multi-user deployments (`3735d324e`)
**Changed (1):** - Make `erk pr teleport` slot-aware, updating slot assignment when teleporting within a slot-assigned worktree (`5b7386776`) - Switch remote dispatch workflows to Sonnet 4.6 (`211f5a0bc`)
**Fixed (4):** - Fix statusline model indicator to correctly display Opus/Sonnet/Haiku with 1M context suffix (`3155e0417`) - Fix `get_ahead_behind` to compare named branch instead of HEAD, preventing incorrect ahead/behind counts (`cc568acbe`) - Fix TUI land command argument mismatch causing error toasts on every land operation (`1a2d56da9`) - Fix erk-mcp error suppression and harden HTTP auth configuration to fail closed (`133e7b398`) — rolls up 3 MCP commits
Approve, or further adjustments?

tool_use.name: Read
tool_use.param: /Users/schrockn/code/erk/CHANGELOG.md

session.tool_result:
1→# Changelog 2→ 3→All notable changes to erk will be documented in this file. 4→ 5→The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/), 6→and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html). 7→ 8→## [Unreleased] 9→ 10→<!-- As of: d5c0167db --> 11→ 12→### Added 13→ 14→- Add `erk json` command namespace providing dedicated machine-readable JSON endpoints for agent and MCP consumption (636ea3d02) 15→- Add `erk workflow run cancel` and `erk workflow run retry` commands plus TUI command palette actions for managing workflow runs (9261f93b1) 16→- Add implementation failure summary posted as PR comment when remote CI fails (9a59f7015) 17→- Add PR status emoji column to Runs tab showing approval state, draft status, and merge conflicts (52067f1ff) 18→- Add `erk json objective view` and `erk json objective check` machine commands (3ec429ddc) 19→- Add `erk stack sync` command for mechanical divergence resolution across Graphite stacks (hidden) (43983f7af) 20→- Add Runs tab to TUI dashboard displaying GitHub Actions workflow runs (963a7574f) 21→- Add branch column to Runs tab with vim-style j/k navigation (10aa81f32) 22→- Add `--json` and MCP exposure to `erk pr list` and `erk pr view` (8bad317c7) 23→- Add prompt validation to one-shot planning workflow to reject invalid prompts (f5c0312ad) 24→ 25→### Changed 26→ 27→- Identify plans by `[erk-pr]`/`[erk-learn]` title prefix instead of `erk-plan` label, simplifying label scheme to two core labels (a024e215e) 28→- Rename `[plans]` config section to `[github]` with backwards compatibility fallback (80f4e949c) 29→- Rename `--plan` to `--pr` across launch/dispatch commands (064a2988e) 30→- Rename plan terminology to PR across core types, gateway interfaces, CLI parameters, JSON output, TUI, and user-facing messages (34ff359af, fe929afcb, 5b8bb600f, 9a193b43c, 63c9de528, 5c628ddd2, bcc0c2d73, 17dfd5e4c)
... [50 lines omitted] ...
37→- Fix prompt passing to claude CLI via stdin to avoid ARG_MAX errors with large prompts (0836187a3) 61→- Add `@json_command` decorator providing structured JSON I/O (`--json` flag) and unified error handling across CLI commands

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: git rev-parse --short HEAD
Get current HEAD short hash

tool_use.name: Write
tool_use.param: /Users/schrockn/.claude/plans/parallel-mapping-hoare.md

tool_use.name: ToolSearch
tool_use.param: select:ExitPlanMode

tool_use.name: ExitPlanMode
tool_use.param:
# Plan: Update CHANGELOG.md Unreleased Section
## Context
CHANGELOG.md unreleased sectio...[truncated 1197 chars]...- Read back the Unreleased section after edits to confirm correct formatting and ordering
/Users/schrockn/.claude/plans/parallel-mapping-hoare.md

session.tool_result:
PreToolUse:ExitPlanMode hook error: [command -v erk >/dev/null 2>&1 || exit 0; ERK_HOOK_ID=exit-plan-mode-hook erk exec exit-plan-mode-hook]: DISPLAY PLAN: Before asking the question below, read the plan file and display its contents to the user with proper markdown formatting: /Users/schrockn/.claude/plans/parallel-mapping-hoare.md
PLAN SAVE PROMPT
A plan exists for this session but has not been saved.
Use AskUserQuestion to ask the user: question: "📋 Plan: Update CHANGELOG.md Unreleased Section Current context: (wt:erk) (br:master)\n\nWhat would you like to do with this plan?" header: "br:master"
IMPORTANT: Present options in this exact order: 1. "Create new branch and planned PR. Stays on current branch." - Create a new branch, save plan as draft PR. 2. "Implement without saving" - Implement directly on the current branch without saving a plan PR. 3. "View/Edit the Plan" - Open plan in editor to review or modify before deciding.
⚠️ WARNING: Currently on 'master'. We strongly discourage implementing directly on the trunk branch. Consider saving the plan and implementing in a dedicated worktree instead.
If user chooses 'Create new branch and planned PR': 1. Run /erk:plan-save 2. Call ExitPlanMode to end the planning session.
If user chooses 'Implement without saving': 1. Create implement-now marker: erk exec marker create --session-id 400e78d2-10a8-4dee-afd0-9430b45be174 \ exit-plan-mode-hook.implement-now 2. Call ExitPlanMode 3. After exiting plan mode, implement the changes directly on the current branch. Do NOT run 'erk exec setup-impl' or create a new branch.
... [8 lines omitted] ...

tool_use.name: Read
tool_use.param: /Users/schrockn/.claude/plans/parallel-mapping-hoare.md

session.tool_result: 1→# Plan: Update CHANGELOG.md Unreleased Section 2→ 3→## Context 4→ 5→CHANGELOG.md unreleased section is synced as of `d5c0167db`. There are 7 new user-facing entries to add from commits merged since then. HEAD is `4637c3bfa`. 6→ 7→## Changes to CHANGELOG.md 8→ 9→1. **Update "As of" marker** on line 10: `d5c0167db` → `4637c3bfa` 10→ 11→2. **Append to `### Added`** (after line 23): 12→ - `- Wire per-user GitHub OAuth token into erk-mcp for authenticated multi-user deployments (3735d324e)` 13→ 14→3. **Append to `### Changed`** (after line 33): 15→ - `- Make `erk pr teleport` slot-aware, updating slot assignment when teleporting within a slot-assigned worktree (5b7386776)` 16→ - `- Switch remote dispatch workflows to Sonnet 4.6 (211f5a0bc)` 17→ 18→4. **Append to `### Fixed`** (after line 40): 19→ - `- Fix statusline model indicator to correctly display Opus/Sonnet/Haiku with 1M context suffix (3155e0417)` 20→ - `- Fix `get_ahead_behind` to compare named branch instead of HEAD, preventing incorrect ahead/behind counts (cc568acbe)` 21→ - `- Fix TUI land command argument mismatch causing error toasts on every land operation (1a2d56da9)` 22→ - `- Fix erk-mcp error suppression and harden HTTP auth configuration to fail closed (133e7b398)` 23→ 24→No new categories needed. No entries removed. Existing entries preserved as-is. 25→ 26→## Verification 27→ 28→- Read back the Unreleased section after edits to confirm correct formatting and ordering 29→

tool_use.name: ToolSearch
tool_use.param: select:AskUserQuestion

**Changes:** 1. Update "As of" marker: `d5c0167db` → `4637c3bfa` 2. Append 1 Added, 2 Changed, 4 Fixed entries under existing category headers

tool_use.name: AskUserQuestion
tool_use.param: [{'question': 'This is a CHANGELOG-only edit on master. What would you like to do?', 'head...[truncated 299 chars]...tion': 'Open plan in editor to review or modify before deciding'}], 'multiSelect': False}]

session.tool_result: User has answered your questions: "This is a CHANGELOG-only edit on master. What would you like to do?"="Create new branch and planned PR". You can now continue with the user's answers in mind.

tool_use.name: Skill
tool_use.param: erk:plan-save

session.user:
# /erk:plan-save
Save the current session's plan to GitHub with session context.
## Usage
```bash /erk:plan-save # Standalone plan /erk:plan-save --plan-type=learn # Learn plan (erk-learn label) /erk:plan-save --objective=123 # Explicit objective link (overrides marker) /erk:plan-save --current-branch # Use current branch instead of creating new plnd/ branch ```
Objective linking: if a plan was created via `/erk:objective-plan`, the session's objective-context marker is read automatically by the save command. Use `--objective=<number>` to override the marker or provide the link when no marker exists (e.g., during replan).
## Plan Storage
The plan is saved using the configured backend:
- **Draft PR backend** (`PLAN_BACKEND = "draft_pr"`): Creates a branch, pushes a plan commit, and opens a draft PR. Plan content is in the PR body after the metadata separator. The JSON output contract includes `pr_number`, `pr_url`, `title`, `branch_name`, `plan_backend`.
## Agent Instructions
### Step 1: Parse Arguments
Check `` for the `--plan-type`, `--objective`, and `--current-branch` flags:
``` If contains "--plan-type=<type>": - Extract the type (standard or learn) - Store as PLAN_TYPE variable - Set PLAN_TYPE_FLAG to "--plan-type=<type>" Else: - Set PLAN_TYPE_FLAG to empty string
If contains "--objective=<number>": - Extract the number - Set OBJECTIVE_FLAG to "--objective=<number>" Else: - Set OBJECTIVE_FLAG to empty string
If contains "--current-branch": - Set CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG to "--current-branch" Else: - Set CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG to empty string ```
### Step 2: Generate Branch Slug
**Skip this step if `--current-branch` was passed.** The current branch is used directly.
Before saving, generate a branch slug from the plan title. Read the plan title from the plan file you wrote in plan mode (the first `# ` heading).
Generate a branch slug from the title:
- 2-4 hyphenated lowercase words, max 30 characters - Capture distinctive essence, drop filler words (the, a, for, implementation, plan) - Prefer action verbs: add, fix, refactor, update, consolidate, extract, migrate - Examples: "fix-auth-session", "add-plan-validation", "refactor-gateway-abc"
Store the result as `BRANCH_SLUG`.
### Step 3: Generate Plan Summary
Write a concise 2-3 sentence summary of the plan. This summary will be visible at the top of the PR description (above the collapsed full plan).
Guidelines:
- Focus on WHAT the plan does and WHY - Do not repeat the title - Plain text, no markdown headers or formatting - Avoid special shell characters (backticks, dollar signs) - Store as PLAN_SUMMARY
### Step 4: Run Save Command
Run this command with the session ID, summary, and optional flags.
If CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG is set, pass `--current-branch` (no branch slug needed):
```bash erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="400e78d2-10a8-4dee-afd0-9430b45be174" --current-branch --summary="${PLAN_SUMMARY}" ${PLAN_TYPE_FLAG} ${OBJECTIVE_FLAG} ```
Otherwise, pass the branch slug:
```bash erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="400e78d2-10a8-4dee-afd0-9430b45be174" --branch-slug="${BRANCH_SLUG}" --summary="${PLAN_SUMMARY}" ${PLAN_TYPE_FLAG} ${OBJECTIVE_FLAG} ```
Parse the JSON output to extract `pr_number` for verification in Step 5.
If the command fails, display the error and stop.
### Step 5: Verify Objective Link (if applicable)
**Only run this step if `objective_issue` is non-null in the JSON output from Step 4.**
Verify the objective link was saved correctly:
```bash erk exec get-plan-metadata <pr_number> objective_issue ```
Parse the JSON response:
- If `success: true` and `value` matches the expected objective number: verification passed - If `success: false` or value doesn't match: verification failed
**On verification success:**
Display: `Verified objective link: #<objective-number>`
**On verification failure:**
Display error and remediation steps:
``` ERROR: Objective link verification failed Expected objective: #<expected> Actual: <actual-or-null>
The plan was saved but without the correct objective link. Fix: Close draft PR #<pr_number>, ensure the objective-context marker exists, and re-run /erk:plan-save. ```
Exit without creating the plan-saved marker. The session continues so the user can retry.
### Step 6: Update Objective Roadmap (if objective linked)
**Only run this step if `objective_issue` was non-null in JSON output and verification passed.**
Update the objective's roadmap table to show that a plan has been created for this node:
1. **Read the roadmap node marker** to get the node ID:
```bash step_id=$(erk exec marker read --session-id "400e78d2-10a8-4dee-afd0-9430b45be174" roadmap-step) ```
If the marker doesn't exist (command fails), skip this step - the plan wasn't created via `objective-plan`.
2. **Update the roadmap table** using the dedicated command:
```bash erk exec update-objective-node <objective-issue> --node "$step_id" --pr "#<pr_number>" --status in_progress ```
This atomically fetches the objective body, finds the matching node row, sets the Status cell to `in-progress`, and writes the updated body back.
3. **Report the update:**
Display: `Updated objective #<objective-issue> roadmap: node <step_id> → PR #<pr_number>`
**Error handling:** If the roadmap update fails, warn but continue - the plan was saved successfully, just the roadmap tracking didn't update. The user can manually update the objective.
### Step 7: Display Results
**If JSON contains `skipped_duplicate: true`:**
Display: `Plan already saved as PR #<pr_number> (duplicate skipped)`
Then call ExitPlanMode. The exit-plan-mode hook will present "what next?" options.
**Otherwise, on success**, display:
``` Plan "<title>" saved as draft PR #<pr_number> URL: <issue_url> ```
If objective was verified, also display: `Verified objective link: #<objective-number>`
Then call ExitPlanMode. The exit-plan-mode hook will present "what next?" options (implement in current worktree, implement in new worktree, or done).
On failure, display the error message and suggest:
- Checking that a plan exists (enter Plan mode and exit it first) - Verifying GitHub CLI authentication (`gh auth status`) - Checking network connectivity
### Step 5: Push Planning Session
After saving successfully, push the planning session for cross-machine learning. This captures the in-progress session state so it's available even if implementation happens on a different machine.
Run `erk exec capture-session-info` to get the session file path, then push it:
```bash erk exec upload-impl-session --session-id "400e78d2-10a8-4dee-afd0-9430b45be174" 2>/dev/null || true ```
If the plan was saved successfully and `pr_number` is known, use push-session directly for better stage tracking:
```bash erk exec push-session \ --session-file "<session_file_path>" \ --session-id "400e78d2-10a8-4dee-afd0-9430b45be174" \ --stage planning \ --source local \ --pr-number <pr_number> \ 2>/dev/null || true ```
Where `<session_file_path>` is obtained from `erk exec capture-session-info` output and `<pr_number>` is from Step 2's JSON output.
**Note:** This is non-critical. If it fails, the plan was still saved successfully. The `2>/dev/null || true` ensures graceful degradation.
## Session Tracking
After successfully saving a plan, the plan number is stored in a marker file that enables automatic plan updates in the same session.
**To read the saved plan number:**
```bash erk exec marker read --session-id "400e78d2-10a8-4dee-afd0-9430b45be174" plan-saved-issue ```
This returns the plan number (exit code 0) or exits with code 1 if no plan was saved in this session.
session.assistant:
Branch slug: `update-changelog-unreleased`
Summary: Adds 7 new user-facing entries to the CHANGELOG.md unreleased section covering erk-mcp OAuth and error handling, TUI fixes, statusline fix, teleport slot awareness, and dispatch model upgrade. Updates the sync marker to current HEAD.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="400e78d2-10a8-4dee-afd0-9430b45be174" --bra...[truncated 174 chars]...port slot awareness, and dispatch model upgrade. Updates the sync marker to current HEAD."
Save plan as draft PR via erk

session.tool_result:
Exit code 1 Traceback (most recent call last): File "/Users/schrockn/code/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/subprocess_utils.py", line 122, in run_subprocess_with_context result = subprocess.run( cmd, ...<8 lines>... **kwargs, ) File "/Users/schrockn/.local/share/uv/python/cpython-3.13.1-macos-aarch64-none/lib/python3.13/subprocess.py", line 577, in run raise CalledProcessError(retcode, process.args, output=stdout, stderr=stderr) subprocess.CalledProcessError: Command '['git', 'fetch', 'origin', 'master']' returned non-zero exit status 128.
The above exception was the direct cause of the following exception:
Traceback (most recent call last): File "/Users/schrockn/code/erk/.venv/bin/erk", line 10, in <module> sys.exit(main()) ~~~~^^ File "/Users/schrockn/code/erk/src/erk/cli/cli.py", line 234, in main cli() ~~~^^ File "/Users/schrockn/code/erk/.venv/lib/python3.13/site-packages/click/core.py", line 1485, in __call__ return self.main(*args, **kwargs) ~~~~~~~~~^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ File "/Users/schrockn/code/erk/.venv/lib/python3.13/site-packages/click/core.py", line 1406, in main rv = self.invoke(ctx) File "/Users/schrockn/code/erk/.venv/lib/python3.13/site-packages/click/core.py", line 1873, in invoke return _process_result(sub_ctx.command.invoke(sub_ctx)) ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^^^^^^^^^
... [49 lines omitted] ...
raise RuntimeError(error_msg) from e RuntimeError: Failed to fetch branch 'master' from remote 'origin' stderr: fatal: unable to access 'https://github.com/dagster-io/erk.git/': Could not resolve host: github.com
session.assistant: Network error — `Could not resolve host: github.com`. Can't save the plan as a draft PR right now.

tool_use.name: AskUserQuestion
